title: Onobroma pectinatum
taxonomy: Onobroma pectinatum belongs to the family Guttiferae, but is now more commonly classified under the family Clusiaceae. This classification change may be due to the reevaluation of plant families in the scientific community. The species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a type of flowering plant with distinctive characteristics. The classification of Onobroma pectinatum is subject to ongoing research and possible updates in the field of botany.
uses: Onobroma pectinatum, also known as the paratodo or umbrella tree, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. The plant's bark and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses of Onobroma pectinatum.
